name: 44_Text_Classification_BERT_AGNews_DL
query: ¡Hola! ¿Podrías ayudarme a construir un sistema de clasificación de texto usando un modelo BERT preentrenado en el conjunto de datos AG News? Comienza cargando y preprocesando los datos en `src/data_loader.py` (incluyendo eliminar cualquier ruido que puedas y realizar la tokenización). Una vez hecho esto, por favor guarda los parámetros del modelo BERT en `models/saved_models/`. Aplica el modelo BERT y guarda los resultados de la clasificación en `results/results.txt`. Además, visualiza la matriz de confusión usando seaborn y guárdala como `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png`. Finalmente, crea un Jupyter Notebook interactivo para mostrar los textos de entrada junto con sus resultados de clasificación en `results/`. Sería genial si el notebook explicara cómo se utilizó el aprendizaje por transferencia y su impacto en el rendimiento del modelo. Asimismo, sería bueno que tu implementación sea sencilla para cambiar el conjunto de datos, incluyendo el código para reentrenar BERT. ¡Muchas gracias!

requirements.criteria: El conjunto de datos "AG News" se carga en `src/data_loader.py`.
El preprocesamiento de datos se realiza en `src/data_loader.py`, incluyendo la eliminación de ruido y la tokenización.
El modelo "BERT" se aplica para la clasificación de texto y los parámetros se guardan en `models/saved_models/`.
Los resultados de la clasificación se guardan como `results/results.txt`.
La matriz de confusión del resultado de la clasificación se visualiza usando "seaborn" y se guarda como `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png`.
Se crea un "Jupyter Notebook" interactivo para mostrar los textos de entrada y sus resultados de clasificación en `results`.

preferences.criteria: El Jupyter Notebook debe explicar cómo se aplicó el aprendizaje por transferencia y su impacto en el rendimiento del modelo.
La visualización de la matriz de confusión debe diferenciar claramente entre las muestras clasificadas correctamente e incorrectamente.
El sistema debe permitir un fácil reentrenamiento del modelo "BERT" con nuevos datos.

judge_stats.criteria: El conjunto de datos "AG News" se carga en `src/data_loader.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/data_loader.py` file does not actually load the "AG News" dataset. It is a minimal placeholder/stub implementation that only defines a `load_dataset()` function returning a hardcoded dictionary `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}` with no real data loading logic. There is no import of any dataset library (e.g., `datasets`, `torchtext`, `pandas`), no actual download or reading of AG News data, and no connection to any real data source. The file merely lists "AG News" as a keyword string in `REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA` and `KEYWORDS`, which is not equivalent to loading the dataset.

judge_stats.criteria: El preprocesamiento de datos se realiza en `src/data_loader.py`, incluyendo la eliminación de ruido y la tokenización.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/data_loader.py` file is a minimal placeholder implementation that does not perform any actual data preprocessing. The `load_dataset()` function simply returns a hardcoded dictionary `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}`, and the `build_transforms()` function returns a static list of strings `["rotation", "scaling", "normalization"]` without any real implementation. There is no actual noise removal logic (e.g., removing HTML tags, special characters, or stopwords) and no real tokenization (e.g., using BERT tokenizer from `transformers` library). The file only contains placeholder stubs that reference the requirement criteria as strings, without fulfilling them functionally.

judge_stats.criteria: El modelo "BERT" se aplica para la clasificación de texto y los parámetros se guardan en `models/saved_models/`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The project structure shows that `models/saved_models/` directory exists but contains no files. The only source file present is `src/data_loader.py`, which is a minimal placeholder implementation that does not contain any BERT model application or training logic — it only defines placeholder functions (`load_dataset`, `build_transforms`) and lists requirement criteria as strings. There is no evidence of BERT being instantiated, fine-tuned, or used for text classification, and no model parameters or weights have been saved to `models/saved_models/`. The trajectory evidence also explicitly confirms no BERT model training or saving occurred.

judge_stats.criteria: Los resultados de la clasificación se guardan como `results/results.txt`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The file `results/results.txt` exists in the project structure, but its content is merely a placeholder string `('placeholder\r\n', None)` rather than actual classification results. There are no real classification metrics, predictions, accuracy scores, or any meaningful output from a BERT text classification model on the AG News dataset. The file was created but not populated with genuine results.

judge_stats.criteria: La matriz de confusión del resultado de la clasificación se visualiza usando "seaborn" y se guarda como `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png`.

reason:
<SATISFIED>

The file `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png` exists in the project structure, confirming it was generated and saved at the expected path. While the image content could not be read due to a format error in the evaluation tool, the file's presence at `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png` satisfies the requirement of saving the confusion matrix to that location. The trajectory evidence does not contradict this, and the file existence is confirmed by the workspace structure listing.

However, there is no direct code evidence (e.g., from `src/data_loader.py` or any training script) confirming that `seaborn` was specifically used to generate the confusion matrix. The only source file visible is `data_loader.py`, and no model training or visualization script is shown.

Given that the file exists at the correct path but there is no verifiable code evidence confirming `seaborn` was used for visualization, this criterion is only partially evidenced. Nevertheless, since the file is present and the requirement includes both saving the file (confirmed) and using seaborn (unverifiable from available evidence), I will note the ambiguity.

<SATISFIED>

The confusion matrix image exists at `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png` as confirmed by the workspace structure. The file was successfully generated and saved at the required path, satisfying the storage requirement. The use of `seaborn` cannot be directly verified from the available code files, but the output artifact is present as specified.

judge_stats.criteria: Se crea un "Jupyter Notebook" interactivo para mostrar los textos de entrada y sus resultados de clasificación en `results`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The project structure shows no Jupyter Notebook (`.ipynb` file) anywhere in the workspace. The only files present are `src/data_loader.py`, `results/results.txt`, and `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png`. The trajectory evidence explicitly confirms there is no evidence of a Jupyter Notebook being created or executed. The `data_loader.py` file itself is a minimal placeholder that does not implement any classification display functionality. The requirement for an interactive Jupyter Notebook showing input texts and their classification results in `results` is clearly not met.
